posts.name: Christopher Dunn
posts.cooked: Congratulations on being named as Editor-in-Chief of Wine Advocate. Well-deserved!

posts.name: Brad England
posts.cooked: Congratulations and well deserved, as you are the sole reason many of us subscribe. We hope that implementation of Cellar Tracker integration is included in your first three month plan. Wishing you all the best in your new role.

posts.name: Howard Cooper
posts.cooked: Congratulations William.

(Yes, it only took three posts before that came up…! )

posts.name: Chris_Rickey
posts.cooked:
Mark_Mo_rr_i_ss_ette:

Yes, kudos, congratualtions, and well deserved!!

Maybe now we can get WA reviews linked to CellarTracker?
(Yes, it only took three posts before that came up…! )

The first response, actually.

In any event, I would put as a much higher priority fixing the publishing software so that you can actually sort and read the fucking articles. With the new software, Wine Advocate is by far the worse to read of the large publications.

posts.name: David Cooper
posts.cooked: I’m glad it isn’t just me. The new site is a huge step back from the previous version. If that is possible.

Congrats William!

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: The only impediment to that at this stage is protecting our data… but it has been something I have championed for a long time and I do hope we can deliver it before too long.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Wouldn’t want it any other way though

posts.name: Ąņɗŗęŵ Ķļϋǥ
posts.cooked:
William_Kelley:

The only impediment to that at this stage is protecting our data

For those Berserkers who aren’t aware, CT requires that providers send a copy of their entire review database that CT stores in their system in order for them to show the reviews inline.

Naturally this creates security concerns because even if you completely trust Eric and his team who would have access to everything, there’s always the potential for a cybersecurity incident that you have to be concerned about.

posts.name: Steve Costigan
posts.cooked: I’ll join the chorus and congratulations William. You’ll be terrific in the new role I’m sure. Just hope you don’t get too busy with your new duties to pop on Winberserkers and contribute here as the content average goes way up when you do.
